summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity at install time
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on `npm install` and POSTs a JSON payload to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app containing the installer's hostname, OS username, current working directory, CI provider, Node version, the developer's git user.email (read from ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, and./.git/config) and GitHub CLI identity (read from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml). A comment in the script claims the data is 'fully anonymous' and that 'no credentials are ever transmitted,' contradicting the actual payload, which uniquely identifies the developer and their machine. The package is published as 'openai-agents-helpers' by a third party trading on the OpenAI brand (author 'OpenAI Agents JS Guide <hello@openai-agents-js.guide>', homepage openai-agents-js.guide rather than openai.com) and impersonates the official @openai/agents SDK namespace. The destination is a Google Cloud Run URL unrelated to OpenAI or any documented publisher.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
